SWEEPING DECISION BY U. s.
SUPREME COURT BASIS FOR


CAMPAIGN AGAINST AMERI-


CAN FEDERATION OF LABOR.

pages.raw_text:
WASHINGTON. d. c., Feb. A.
AS a result of the sweeping anti
boycott decision by the U. S. SUI
preme court movement has al
ready been started among manu-
facturers who are represented on
the unfair" list of the American
Federation of Labor to appeal to
Roosevelt for relief.


Daniel Davenport, counsel for
the Loewe Co., complainant in the
case decided yesterday, is author
ity for a statement that the matter
will be brought to the presidents
attention in the near future. He
points out paragraph in the re-
cent presidential message in which
Roosevelt declared he would en-
force the law against labor unions
as quickly as against corporations.


Let us cease fighting. Let or
ganized labor and capital go for
ward in peace. Now that the SUI
